title: PUBLISH
description: Posts a message to a channel.
complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of clients subscribed to the receiving channel and M is the total number of subscribed patterns (by any client).

params.description: Channel to publish the message to
params.name: channel

returns.description: Number of clients that received the message

params.description: Channel to publish the message to
params.name: channel

returns.description: Number of clients that received the message

jedis.signature: long publish(final byte[] channel, final byte[] message)

params.description: Channel to publish the message to
params.name: channel

returns.description: Number of clients that received the message

jedis.signature: long publish(final String channel, final String message)

params.description: Channel to publish the message to
params.name: channel

returns.description: Number of clients that received the message

go-redis.signature: Publish(ctx context.Context, channel string, message interface{})

params.description: The flags to use for this operation
params.name: flags

returns.description: The number of clients that received the message on the destination server

nredisstack_sync.signature: long Publish(RedisChannel channel, RedisValue message, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

params.description: The flags to use for this operation
params.name: flags

returns.description: The number of clients that received the message on the destination server

nredisstack_async.signature: Task<long> PublishAsync(RedisChannel channel, RedisValue message, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

params.description: Channel to publish the message to
params.name: $channel

returns.description: Number of clients that received the message
number of clients that received the message

content:
Posts a message to the given channel. In a Redis Cluster clients can publish to every node. The cluster makes sure that published messages are forwarded as needed, so clients can subscribe to any channel by connecting to any one of the nodes. ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="publish-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of clients that the message was sent to. Note that in a Redis Cluster, only clients that are connected to the same node as the publishing client are included in the count. -tab-sep- [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of clients that the message was sent to. Note that in a Redis Cluster, only clients that are connected to the same node as the publishing client are included in the count. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Posts a message to the given channel.

In a Redis Cluster clients can publish to every node. The cluster makes sure
that published messages are forwarded as needed, so clients can subscribe to any
channel by connecting to any one of the nodes.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Posts a message to the given channel.

In a Redis Cluster clients can publish to every node. The cluster makes sure
that published messages are forwarded as needed, so clients can subscribe to any
channel by connecting to any one of the nodes.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="publish-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of clients that the message was sent to. Note that in a Redis Cluster, only clients that are connected to the same node as the publishing client are included in the count.

-tab-sep-

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of clients that the message was sent to. Note that in a Redis Cluster, only clients that are connected to the same node as the publishing client are included in the count.

{{< /multitabs >}}
